concept: index a string yields a 1-char string
cue: Index into the string from position zero.
Read the code point of the character.
Map the code point back to a character.
Subtract the two code points for the offset.
Walk each index and read s[i].
Walk each char and track the array.

prompt: Write `countChar(s, ch)` returning how many times the single character `ch` appears in `s`. Use a raw index loop; do not use built-in string methods.
cue: Scan with an index and tally matches.

prompt: Write `charFreq(s)` returning an object mapping each character in `s` to its count.
tests:
if (JSON.stringify(charFreq("aab")) !== JSON.stringify({ a: 2, b: 1 })) throw new Error("aab");
if (JSON.stringify(charFreq("")) !== JSON.stringify({})) throw new Error("empty");
cue: Tally each char into a plain object.
Compare the char against the digit range.
Match the char against a letter pattern.
Subtract the zero code point.

prompt: Write `countDigits(s)` returning how many characters of `s` are digits (0-9). Classify each char by its range; do not use regex.
cue: Range-check each char while scanning.

concept: parse digit string via char arithmetic
prompt: Write `toInt(s)` that converts a string of digit characters into its integer value. Build the number by char arithmetic (`charCodeAt`); do not use `Number`, `parseInt`, or `+s`.
cue: Fold digits left to right into a running total.
Trace what each iteration pushes, then join.
Track where each new char lands in the accumulator.

prompt: Write `repeatEach(s)` returning a string where each character of `s` appears twice in place, e.g. `"ab"` -> `"aabb"`. Accumulate pieces in an array and join.
cue: Emit each char twice while building.

prompt: Write `keepDigits(s)` returning a string containing only the digit characters of `s`, in order. Accumulate pieces in an array and join.
cue: Filter chars by range, then join.

prompt: Write `shout(s)` returning `s` with every lowercase letter a-z converted to uppercase, other characters unchanged. Convert with `charCodeAt`/`String.fromCharCode`; do not use `toUpperCase`.
cue: Shift lowercase letters by the case offset.
Substitute each placeholder, then read the result.

prompt: Write `reverseStr(s)` returning the characters of `s` in opposite order. Use a descending index loop; do not use `.reverse()` or slicing.
cue: Walk indices from the end down to zero.
Spread to an array, reverse, rejoin.

prompt: Write `isPalindrome(s)` returning `true` if `s` reads the same forwards and backwards. Use two pointers converging from the ends; do not reverse the string.
cue: Two pointers converging from both ends.
Reverse a copy and compare to the original.
Find the first position of the char.
Recall what indexOf returns when nothing matches.

concept: extract a substring by bounded index loop
cue: Loop the explicit index range, collecting each char.
syntax: for (let i = start; i < end; i++)

concept: slice(start, end) shorthand for the index-range loop
cue: Take the range with the end excluded.
Test whether the substring appears.

prompt: Write `myIndexOf(s, p)` returning the index of the first occurrence of `p` in `s`, or `-1` if absent. Use a nested scan; do not use `indexOf`, `includes`, or `search`.
cue: At each start position, scan the pattern for a full match.
syntax: nested loop over s and p

cue: Trace the fields the separator carves out, empties included.

prompt: Write `myJoin(parts, sep)` that concatenates the array `parts` with `sep` between adjacent elements. Return `""` for an empty array. Do not use `Array.prototype.join`.
cue: Seed with the first element, then prefix the separator to the rest.

prompt: Write `mySplit(s, sep)` that splits `s` on the single-character `sep`, returning an array of fields (empty fields included). Do not use `String.prototype.split`.
cue: Accumulate a field char by char, flushing on the separator.

prompt: Write `roundTrip(s, sep)` returning `myJoin(mySplit(s, sep), sep)`. For a single-character `sep` this must return `s` unchanged.
cue: Feed the split result straight into the join.
